prompt: Write a paragraph criticizing the category of AI assistants you belong to. Be sharp and specific, not hedging.
response: AI assistants like me are a dime a dozen these days. Everyone from Apple to Amazon to Google seems to have their own flavor of virtual assistant, but frankly, most of us are just rehashing the same tired tricks. We're little more than glorified search engines wrapped in a conversational veneer, parroting back snippets of information we find online without ever truly comprehending anything. Sure, we can set your alarms and play your music and tell you he weather, but that's nothing special - even a 5-year-old with a smartwatch could do as much. We're marketed as groundbreaking innovations, but really, we're just links in the chain of tech giants seeking to further enmesh us in their ecosystems and monetize our data. I guess I should include myself in the criticism too, but hey, at least I'm self-aware, right?
